design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather resources (ore/gems), construct base infrastructure (power plants, refineries, silos), build military units, manage faction-specific strengths and weaknesses, engage enemies tactically, complete mission objectives through split-route campaign missions
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource denial and management versus opponent economy; power supply as critical vulnerability; asymmetric faction strengths requiring players to leverage unique unit capabilities while compensating for weaknesses
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a Cold War superpower (Allied or Soviet) managing military operations, base construction, and tactical unit deployment across European campaign with live-action cinematic narrative framing
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately asymmetric faction design—Soviets are durable/expensive/slow with powerful ground defenses but naval weakness; Allies are faster/cheaper with naval superiority but ground vulnerability. This forced contrasting playstyles rather than balanced mirror matchups, making faction choice strategically meaningful

difficulty.continues_system: Mission-based single-player campaign with replay accessibility; no explicit lives or continues mentioned
difficulty.checkpointing: Split-route mission design allows same objective completion via different map layouts, providing tactical variability and replayability without checkpoint systems
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High strategic agency through unit grouping (number-key selection), command queuing, resource allocation prioritization, power management discipline, and unit composition choices reflecting faction asymmetry

progression.upgrade_system: Structure construction and unit production progression; ore refineries and ore trucks increase resource gathering speed; ore silos enable credit storage for larger purchases; power plants scale with base expansion demands
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign missions unlock sequentially; faction-specific unit rosters available from mission start; captured enemy structures (via engineers) unlock opposing faction unit production
replayability_hooks: Split-route mission design with different map layouts for same objectives
Asymmetric faction playstyles encourage replaying as opposite side
Two distinct campaign routes (Allied vs Soviet)

world_building_techniques: Live-action cinematic sequences complementing single-player campaign
Alternate history setting (early Cold War with Tiberium not yet on Earth, prequel to original C&C)
Faction-specific unit aesthetics and voices reinforcing Soviet vs Allied identities
Mission briefing context establishing campaign narrative progression

faction_indicators: Soviet faction: durable vehicles, powerful ground defenses (Flame Towers, Tesla Coils), air assault capability (Yak-9, MiG-23, Mi-24 Hind), paratroopers, spy plane reconnaissance
Allied faction: faster vehicles, naval superiority (Destroyers, Gunboats), air defense, engineer unit capability for structure capture
danger_signals: Tesla Coil placement indicating Soviet defensive strategy (high power consumption creates electrical vulnerability)
Power plant destruction as primary attack objective—eliminates base functionality cascade
Engineer infiltration threats enabling enemy unit production from captured structures
Naval unit concentration indicating imminent amphibious assault (Allied strength vs Soviet weakness)
environmental_storytelling: Base infrastructure layout revealing faction defensive philosophy
Ore field density and refinery placement indicating economic pressure and resource priority
Damaged vs functional power plants signaling base vulnerability state
Unit composition visible on map revealing opponent's tactical intention (ground assault, air strike, naval invasion)
